item.title: Herbosa, 16 other DOH officials face graft complaints over P1.4-B expired, undelivered meds
item.description: A group of healthcare advocates filed a graft complaint before the Office of the Ombudsman against Department of Health (DOH) Secretary Teodoro Herbosa and 16 other DOH officials over the alleged wastage of more than P1.4 billion worth of medications.

item.title: 'Bayanihan' type supplemental budget sought amid Middle East war
item.description: Sen. Francis "Kiko" Pangilinan is pursuing a "Bayanihan" type supplemental budget to address the needs of Filipinos who are reeling from the effects of expensive fuel driven by the attacks of the United States and Israel against Iran.

item.title: AFP: China exploiting Middle East conflict to intensify West PH Sea activities
item.description: The Philippine military said that China could be taking advantage of the international attention on the conflict in the Middle East, noting an apparent escalation in harassment activities in the West Philippine Sea, now targeting Philippine grey ships.

item.title: Marcos Jr. to spend Holy Week 2026 in Ilocos Norte
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. will spend this year’s Holy Week in his home province of Ilocos Norte, Malacañang said Tuesday, shortly before the Malacañang ordered half-day work-from-home scheme on April 1.

item.title: Ombudsman, DOJ 'overwhelmed' by ICI case recommendations: Malacañang
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman and the Department of Justice (DOJ) “seem to be overwhelmed” by the volume of case recommendations and information submitted by the Independent Commission for Infrastructure (ICI), Malacañang said Tuesday.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
